ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flagged. Chaos Tower is a TCG-original name with no canonical game-location correspondence verified through available sources.

trivia_bullets.text: Chaos Tower is an Uncommon Stadium card in Fates Collide (XY10, May 2, 2016), the English localization of Japan's Awakening of Psychic Kings. Set marquee cards are Alakazam-EX, M Alakazam-EX, and Zygarde-EX. No flavor text on the card.

Despite the dramatic name, 'Chaos Tower' does not correspond to any named location in the mainline Pokemon games as of research date. The Japanese card name is Chaos Tower (katakana: カオスタワー), carried directly into English. Initial hypothesis that it references Hoopa Unbound (a prominent Fates Collide pull, nicknamed 'the Mischief Pokemon') is unverified — Hoopa's associated canonical location is Dahara City from the 2015 film, not a tower. The name appears to be an original TCG invention for this set.
sources: WebSearch: Bulbapedia Chaos Tower Fates Collide 94
WebSearch: Pokebeach Fates Collide set discussion

trivia_bullets.text: Chaos Tower is one of the TCG's few dual-facing Stadium cards: the player physically chooses which direction to place it, each orientation granting a different Special Condition immunity (Confused/Poisoned vs. Asleep/Paralyzed). The card received a Staff Promo print for the 2016 National Championship, indicating official tournament spotlight use.
sources: WebSearch: spankyscardshop.com Chaos Tower National Championship Promo Staff

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by 5ban Graphics, a studio (not a solo artist) responsible for the majority of EX, Full Art, and Stadium illustrations in the XY and Sun/Moon eras. Their high-contrast prismatic style is the visual signature of XY-era EX cards.

related_cards.name: Alakazam-EX
related_cards.note: Marquee EX of the same set; psychic-theme complement

related_cards.name: Zygarde-EX
related_cards.note: The other headliner; order-vs-chaos thematic counterpart

related_cards.name: Parallel City
related_cards.note: Another dual-direction Stadium card in the XY era; closest mechanical relative
